title: Bootstrap minimal Mix project and kernel OTP shell
description:
Add a root Mix project and a compilable OTP application under kernel/ per AGENTS repository layout.

Scope: mix.exs at repo root path-depending on kernel/, kernel/mix.exs with OTP Application + empty supervisor subtree, minimal config/, so that mix deps.get and mix compile succeed. No caps.toml edits, no capability dirs yet, no business logic.

Out of scope: CapabilityRegistry/CapabilityStorage implementation, caps manifests, infra changes.

title: add local Podman Compose skeleton
description:
Why this issue exists

The README documents a local-first development environment where PostgreSQL, Vault-compatible secrets, Prometheus, Grafana, and the app run through Podman. The repository status says the application scaffold and infra/local/compose.yml still need to be created. This issue captures the first reviewable local infrastructure step without changing production infrastructure or capability state.

Scope

Create the initial local infrastructure skeleton under infra/local/ so future work can run the same service topology locally that production expects at the service level.

In scope

- Add infra/local/compose.yml with local-only service definitions or placeholders for postgres, vault, prometheus, grafana, and app.
- Add or align minimal local start/stop entrypoints so README commands make sense, preferably make dev and make dev-down if the Makefile already owns local workflow.
- Use README-documented local endpoints and environment values: DATABASE_URL, VAULT_ADDR, VAULT_TOKEN, and CAPABILITY_ENV.
- Keep the change small enough for the project <=100 non-generated line commit rule.

Out of scope

- Do not modify AGENTS.md, caps.toml, or caps.lock.
- Do not modify kernel/ or create capability code.
- Do not modify infra/provider.tf or infra/envs/prod/.
- Do not run tofu apply or tofu destroy.
- Do not commit secrets, credentials, generated state, or .tfstate files.
design: Follow README.md as the user-facing contract for local commands and endpoints. Follow AGENTS.md section 14: local development uses Podman Compose and mirrors production service topology; agents may work in infra/local but must not touch production Terraform or run apply/destroy operations. This issue intentionally starts with the smallest useful local infra slice before adding dashboards, app scaffold details, or capability-specific infra.
acceptance_criteria:
- infra/local/compose.yml exists and defines the expected local service topology from README.md.
- Local app service wiring includes DATABASE_URL, VAULT_ADDR, VAULT_TOKEN, and CAPABILITY_ENV using dev-only values.
- make dev and make dev-down either work or the issue result clearly documents the equivalent commands.
- No production Terraform, capability manifest, kernel, or generated state files are changed.
- The resulting commit remains within the <=100 non-generated line limit unless a human-approved exception exists.
notes: Current README.md says infra/local/compose.yml is still missing and lists expected local endpoints: app :4000, postgres :5432, vault :8200, prometheus :9090, grafana :3000. Use local-only default credentials documented there: postgres app/app and Vault dev token dev-root-token.
